message:
fix(fr-command): CLUSTER ADDSLOTSRANGE/SET-CONFIG-EPOCH check ordering matches upstream [bd-frankenredis-ujdy]

Upstream cluster.c::clusterCommand checks server.cluster_enabled
FIRST (replying 'This instance has cluster support disabled'),
THEN dispatches to subcommand handlers for further validation.
The table-level commands.def arity check fires BEFORE clusterCommand.

Two ordering issues fixed:

1. CLUSTER ADDSLOTSRANGE/DELSLOTSRANGE: commands.def declares
   arity = -4. The multiple-of-2 pair check happens inside the
   subcommand handler, AFTER the cluster-enabled gate. fr was
   running the pair check at the top so 'CLUSTER ADDSLOTSRANGE
   1 2 3' returned wrong-arity instead of 'cluster support
   disabled'. Split the check: argc<4 → wrong-arity, then
   cluster-disabled, then odd-pair check.

2. CLUSTER SET-CONFIG-EPOCH: commands.def declares arity = 3.
   fr emitted 'cluster support disabled' for any argc, but
   upstream emits 'wrong number of arguments for
   cluster|set-config-epoch command' for missing/extra args.
   Validate argc==3 before the cluster-disabled error.

CLUSTER SLOTSTATE keeps the unconditional cluster-disabled
fallback since it has no commands.def entry.
